Page.Width: {Binding RelativeSource={RelativeSource Mode=FindAncestor, AncestorType={x:Type ContentControl}}, Path=ActualWidth, FallbackValue=700}
Page.Height: {Binding RelativeSource={RelativeSource Mode=FindAncestor, AncestorType={x:Type ContentControl}}, Path=ActualHeight, FallbackValue=400}

Run.Text: Let's return to our ship and move the vessel using Direct Control first.

All vessels and submarines in Sea Power use an Engine Order Telegraph (EOT) control scheme. This means a ship or submarine can move at the following speeds: Back Full, All Stop, Ahead 1/3, Ahead 2/3, Ahead Standard, Ahead Full, and Ahead Flank.

Ahead Standard usually means to proceed forward at the most economical and fuel efficient speed for that ship type. Ahead Full usually means to proceed forward at 75% of maximum speed, and Ahead Flank means to proceed forward at 100% of maximum speed.
